svg.title: Kerberoasting attack flow
svg.desc: Diagram showing how Kerberoasting abuses legitimate TGS requests to extract service ticket hashes for offline cracking

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the attacker authenticate to get a TGT before Kerberoasting?')
sendPrompt('What does a TGT contain and why does the attacker need it for Kerberoasting?')
sendPrompt('How does an attacker enumerate SPNs in Active Directory?')
sendPrompt('What is a Service Principal Name (SPN) and which accounts have them?')
sendPrompt('Why can any authenticated user request a TGS for any SPN?')
sendPrompt('What key does the KDC use to encrypt the TGS and why is that exploitable?')
sendPrompt('What is returned in the TGS-REP that makes Kerberoasting possible?')
sendPrompt('What does the Kerberoastable hash look like and which hashcat mode cracks it?')
sendPrompt('How do you crack Kerberoast hashes with hashcat and what modes apply?')
sendPrompt('What is the difference between RC4 and AES Kerberoast hashes and which is easier to crack?')
sendPrompt('What can an attacker do after cracking a Kerberoast hash?')
g.text: Authenticate as svc acct → lateral movement, DA escalation if svc is privileged
